intro: The pivotal question between Camunda and Azure OpenAI Service is how much of your setup stays portable: Camunda carries low lock-in and Azure OpenAI Service carries medium lock-in. Camunda is BPMN workflow and decision automation engine for orchestrating APIs and services. Azure OpenAI Service is Azure-hosted OpenAI models with enterprise compliance and regional endpoints. Pick Camunda when the job is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept low lock-in; pick Azure OpenAI Service when it is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Camunda at some public pricing / roadmap detail with a polished developer experience, and Azure OpenAI Service at mostly transparent pricing and docs with solid developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Camunda is a poor fit for internal, simple projects, and Azure OpenAI Service is a poor fit for internal, simple projects. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Camunda is for BPMN workflow and decision automation engine; Azure OpenAI Service is for Azure-hosted OpenAI models; decide based on how portable the setup must stay.
verdict_a: Choose Camunda if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work maps to BPMN workflow and decision automation engine for orchestrating APIs and services.
verdict_b: Choose Azure OpenAI Service if you need public pricing and docs before committing and the work lines up with Azure-hosted OpenAI models with enterprise compliance and regional endpoints.

faq.q: Is Camunda cheaper than Azure OpenAI Service?
faq.a: Camunda starts at $100 on a subscription model; Azure OpenAI Service starts at $1 on a subscription model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Camunda to Azure OpenAI Service?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Camunda carries low lock-in and Azure OpenAI Service carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Camunda scores higher on developer experience in our data (5/5 vs 4/5). That reflects public feedback on docs, onboarding, and day-to-day ergonomics. Still, run a spike with your own code — DX ratings don't capture every edge.

faq.q: Is Azure OpenAI Service a good alternative to Camunda?
faq.a: Yes — Azure OpenAI Service is a reasonable alternative to Camunda for api gateway and rate limiting. The practical differences are subscription-vs-subscription billing and low-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Azure OpenAI Service as a credible swap.
